_meta.note: Starts EMPTY. The system writes lessons here after each human correction (the Reflexion episodic buffer). Top-K (K=3) most relevant lessons are injected into the Actor prompt at answer time. See DESIGN.md section 5 for the schema. The object below is a commented example only and is NOT loaded.

example_lesson.trigger_pattern: why was <release|deploy> delayed / why did <service> fail or slow down
example_lesson.what_ai_missed: Reported the symptom (error/latency spike) and stopped; never traced it to the triggering commit or the violated guideline.
example_lesson.reasoning_rule: For 'why did X fail/get delayed' questions, trace the full causal chain: symptom -> the commit/migration that introduced it (search commits near the incident date) -> whether it violated a documented guideline (search the wiki) -> the fix.
example_lesson.evidence_rule: Cross-reference four source types: incident ticket + Slack incident thread + triggering commit + relevant wiki guideline. An answer citing only Slack is incomplete.
example_lesson.confidence_adjustment: If no triggering commit is found, lower confidence and say so rather than asserting a root cause.

lessons.trigger_pattern: why did X fail or get delayed
lessons.what_ai_missed: specific commit details and explicit reference to migration guidelines
lessons.reasoning_rule: start from the symptom, trace to the triggering code change, check for guideline violations, and confirm the fix and re-release
lessons.evidence_rule: always cross-reference commit history, migration guidelines, and release notes
lessons.confidence_adjustment: lower confidence when specific commit details or guideline references are missing
lessons.created_from_question: Why was the v4.2.0 release delayed?

lessons.what_ai_missed: The AI missed evidence such as SLK-010 and NW-1120, which provided additional context to the issue and its resolution.
lessons.reasoning_rule: Start from the symptom, trace to the triggering code change, check for guideline violations, and confirm the fix
lessons.evidence_rule: Always cross-reference service call guidelines, code change logs, and relevant issue tickets
lessons.confidence_adjustment: Lower confidence when symptoms are complex or multifaceted, and when recent code changes or updates are involved
lessons.created_from_question: Why did payments-gateway latency spike on June 10, and what was the real cause?

lessons.trigger_pattern: troubleshooting service ownership and technical issues
lessons.what_ai_missed: The AI missed providing the specific team of the service owner and the technical root cause of stuck inventory syncs.
lessons.reasoning_rule: Look up the service in the service catalog for owner and team information, and corroborate with historical Q&A records for similar technical issues.
lessons.evidence_rule: Always cross-reference the service catalog and historical Q&A records (e.g. Slack) for service ownership and technical issue resolution.
lessons.confidence_adjustment: Lower confidence when the question requires specific team or technical root cause information that may not be readily available in the training data.
lessons.created_from_question: Who owns inventory-svc and who should I contact about a stuck inventory sync?

lessons.trigger_pattern: why did we choose X over Y
lessons.what_ai_missed: The AI missed additional key reasons for choosing Postgres, including support for relational joins and existing team expertise, as well as corroborating evidence from Slack discussions.
lessons.reasoning_rule: Find the relevant Architecture Decision Record (ADR), summarize the rationale and rejected alternatives, and corroborate with relevant team discussions and documentation.
lessons.evidence_rule: Always cross-reference with ADRs, team discussion logs (e.g. Slack), and relevant documentation for similar technology choices.
lessons.confidence_adjustment: Lower confidence when the question requires knowledge of specific internal discussions, team expertise, or nuanced technical tradeoffs.
lessons.created_from_question: Why did we choose Postgres over DynamoDB for the orders database?
